mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% signals persistent demand pressures that push central banks to maintain tight monetary policy, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 1.5% in 75% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or inflation persistence fails to correlate with rate hike expectations (R-squared < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment (UNRATE) and increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are negative in 60% of cases where both UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises, or the joint effect size falls below 0.15 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average indicates weakening supply chain capacity, creating deflationary risks that drive S&P 500 forward returns negative over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.2% in 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 3-month MA, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns drops below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million units signals robust private sector demand that drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 65% of cases where HOUST exceeds 1.3M, or the predictive power of HOUST drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant money supply growth (RSAFS) indicates monetary policy ineffectiveness, creating uncertainty that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 55% of cases where CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS stagnates, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.1) in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in unemployment (UNRATE) and decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals economic contraction, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 50% of cases where UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls, or the joint effect size drops below 0.10 in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 6-month trend indicates strong supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 55% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 6-month MA, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns drops below 0.10.
